name: SERTOLI-STROMAL CELL TUMOR OF OVARY

demographics.Age: any age; most common in 2nd-3rd decade
demographics.Histo: components of Sertoli cells, Leydig cells, fibroblasts; tissues are so varied that it is
demographics.Mean age: 25 (range, 15-66) years; 75% occur in patients < 30 years of age
demographics.Path: solid ± cystic areas; hemorrhage is rare

dominant_finding: predominantly low SI (= fibroblasts) + scattered areas of high SI on T2WI
discriminating_features: small mass often difficult to visualize by US / CT
solid mass ± cystic components ← hemorrhage and necrosis

general: small mass often difficult to visualize by US / CT
solid mass ± cystic components ← hemorrhage and necrosis
MR: predominantly low SI (= fibroblasts) + scattered areas of high SI on T2WI

clinical.Prognosis: good when detected at stage I (in 92%)
